43779.description: Belvedere Boutique Hotel is located in the serene area of Ludwigsdorf, Klein Windhoek. Nearby attractions include the National Botanical Garden and the Christuskirche. The hotel offers individually designed rooms equipped with air-conditioning, TVs, and Wi-Fi. Guests can enjoy a complimentary breakfast each morning. Leisure facilities include a splash pool, a beauty salon, and a tennis court.

rooms.description: Our Standard Room are furnished modern but elegant, providing the perfect retreat for the business or leisure traveler. The room offers a double bed, coffee/tea station, air conditioner, TV, safe, hair dryer and mini bar
rooms.name: Standard Rooms

rooms.description: Our Luxury Rooms are spacious, furnished modern but elegant and overlook the lavish hotel garden. The room offers a queen size bed, coffee/tea station, air conditioner, TV, hair dryer and safe.
rooms.name: Luxury Rooms

rooms.description: Our Family Room are furnished modern but elegant, providing the perfect retreat for families. There are two bedrooms, one with a queen size bed and the other offers a bunk bed for two children
rooms.name: Family Room

rooms.description: Our Penthouse Suite is spacious, furnished modern but elegant and overlooks the lavish hotel garden. The room is fitted with a king size bed, Espresso coffee machine, air conditioner, hair dryer and safe.
rooms.name: Penthouse

activities.description: The 37 000-hectare (91 429-acre) private Kulala Wilderness Reserve offers visitors exclusive wildlife-viewing opportunities, which include the likes of gemsbok (oryx), bat-eared fox, brown hyena and birdlife. A highlight is a coffee or sundowner stop in the exceptionally scenic reserve.
activities.name: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge – Nature Drives

activities.description: World-famous Sossusvlei is both the name for the area in general; as well as an enormous clay pan, flanked by immense sand dunes. Climb the 300-metre red dunes and gaze upon the famed Namib Sand Sea World Heritage Site. Nearby, at the foot of some of these dunes, the skeletons of 900-year old camelthorn trees dominate the landscape where once water flowed. This is known as Dead Vlei. An exclusive-use gate provides direct access at sunrise to the renowned dunes of Sossusvlei and the evocative panoramas of Dead Vlei.
activities.name: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge – Visit Sossusvlei & Dead Vlei

activities.description: This fascinating geographical formation is well worth a visit. The result of millions of years of water carving its way into the earth, today Sesriem Canyon is three kilometres long. Views from the top are breathtaking, while walks through the canyon reveal distinct geographical layers which were laid down over aeons.
activities.name: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge – Visit Sesriem Canyon

activities.description: Walking trails focus on becoming immersed in this remote area, as well as its scenic beauty and the fascinating, and surprisingly, diverse array of insects, reptiles and rodents which make this dry and harsh region their home. The Tsauchab River Trail takes guests on a stroll along the dry riverbed close by the camp.
activities.name: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge – Guided Nature Trails

activities.description: Set out in the late afternoon for a nature drive, and complete your excursion in a remote and dramatic spot on the reserve, sipping a gin and tonic while watching the sun set over the distant mountains or sweeping dunes.
activities.name: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge – Scenic Sundowner Excursion

activities.description: Ideal for introducing your family to the natural wonders of Namibia, take in the sweeping scenery on fat-wheeled e-bikes. A fun way to explore the spectacular open plains with your guide.
activities.name: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge – Guided Fat-Wheeled e-Bike Excursions

activities.description: Private rooftop area for sleep-outs accessed by a wooden staircase behind each tented guest room. Sleep-out under the twinkling skies in the Namib Desert, and be lulled to sweet slumber by the magical night sounds of Africa.
activities.name: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge – Sleep-Out Experience

activities.description:
The hot air balloon safari on the Kulala Wilderness Reserve offers a truly exceptional experience – soaring silently above the magnificent sand sea of the Sossusvlei dunes, with a champagne breakfast served at your landing site.

- Optional activity at extra cost, and weather permitting.
- Unavailable for the periods 15 January to 15 February (inclusive), and 25 December and 01 January.
activities.name: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge – Hot Air Balloon Excursion *

1520.description:
Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge’s location at the foot of the majestic Sossusvlei dunes allows for stunning views in every direction, while its private entrance to the Namib Naukluft National Park makes it the closest location to Sossusvlei. The camp is ideal for self-drives and groups.

Twenty-one thatched and canvas tents (including two family units), each built on raised wooden platforms make for airy, comfortable accommodation, with a rooftop area for sleep-outs under the stars. Afternoons around the pool provide relief from the heat, while the wraparound veranda at the main area overlooks a waterhole, and private tours of the dunes provide the ultimate Sossusvlei experience.

INSIDER'S TIP: Try your hand at desert photography – whether capturing the red colour of the dunes in the early morning light or the otherworldly tree skeletons standing starkly across the floor of Dead Vlei.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: The comfortable and elegant main area lounge opens onto sweeping views of the Kulala Wilderness Reserve.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: The guest tents have been built on raised wooden platforms to maximise their expansive views.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Can you even say you've been to Kulala Desert Lodge if you haven't climbed Big Daddy dune?

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Take a nature drive to explore the otherworldly landscapes around the lodge.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: The firepit invites convivial pre-dinner drinks.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Explore the reserve on a fat-wheeled e-bike, before stopping for a welcome sundowner.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Dead Vlei is an enormous clay pan, flanked by the immense sand dunes of Sossusvlei.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: A Kulala balloon safari offers the truly unique experience of soaring above the magnificent Namib Desert landscape.  Activity at extra cost and closed on 25 December and 01 January, and from mid-January to mid-February.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: An e-bike ride is a fun way to explore the spectacular open plains with your guide.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Kulala Desert Lodge's lounge area is elegance personified.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: The generous proportions of the main area mimic the sweeping vistas over the Kulala Wilderness Reserve.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Unobtrusive earth tones define the decor in the main area.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Explore the ancient dunes around Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: The lodge's main area was completely rebuilt in early 2024, with modern stretch canvas tents replacing the previously thatched roofs.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Light and airy neutral tones lend a fresh sense of place to the main area.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Appetising treats are a feature of every menu offering.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Enjoy a tasty lunch feast outside on the deck.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: The granite bar-top adds a sophisticated touch to this public space.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: The thatch-and-canvas guest tents are spacious, with roomy decks for private dining, or a quiet pre-dinner drink.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Down-to-Earth Kulala Desert Lodge is comfortable, and perfect for groups, families, and self-drive guests.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Dead Vlei is arguably the most photogenic landmark in an area bristling with extraordinary natural beauty.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Discover an abundance of desert life on a guided nature walk.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: The inviting pool area beckons for sundowners, or a refreshing dip.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: A special occasion is celebrated over a romantic private dinner.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: A private table for two in the desert.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: Start your evening with a fascinating tasting of fine South African wine from our cellar.

images.label: Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
images.description: There are a number of exceptional spots to celebrate with a loved one over a private dinner.

rooms.description: The camp comprises nineteen elegant and comfortable brick and canvas tents under thatch, with twin accommodation and en-suite facilities. Each has been built on a raised wooden platform to maximise the expansive views. Gravel pathways link the tents to the convivial main area. A snug and comfortable private rooftop area for sleep-outs under the stars completes each room, and makes for an unforgettable experience.
rooms.name: Standard Twin/Double Tent(s)

rooms.description: Two spacious family units each have two bedrooms with two three-quarter beds per bedroom, and own en-suite facilities. Separate entrances lead to each family unit, while a shared outdoor deck connects both units.
rooms.name: Family Accommodation

228293.description: Atlantic Garden Boutique Hotel is located in the centre of Swakopmund, offering a fresh and feel-good atmosphere. The accommodation features modern rooms with air conditioning, TVs, and complimentary Wi-Fi. Guests can enjoy dining at the on-site restaurant, which serves breakfast, light lunches, and coffee with cake. Nearby attractions include The Mole Beach and the historical Altes Amtsgericht building, both within walking distance. For leisure, the hotel provides a beautiful garden to relax in after an action-packed day, ensuring a comfortable and enjoyable stay.

rooms.description: Stylishly furnished 2 sleeper twin room. The rooms are equipped with air-conditioning, safe, TV/DStv, small fridge (not stocked) and Tea & coffee station. All bathrooms are fitted with a shower and hairdryer. Wi-Fi is available throughout the hotel.
rooms.name: Twin Room

rooms.description: Stylishly furnished 4 sleeper family room. The rooms are equipped with air-conditioning, safe, TV/DStv, small fridge (not stocked) and Tea & coffee station. All bathrooms are fitted with a shower and hairdryer. Wi-Fi is available throughout the hotel.
rooms.name: Family Room

activities.description: This adrenaline filled morning will give you an experience that will form part of you. Experience coming up close to the wild white Rhino and get a sense of these majestic animals.
activities.name: Rhino Tracking

activities.description: This game drive offers the opportunity to spot 25 different animal species. After an adventurous couple of hours, you will end the drive on one of our scenic mountain tops.
activities.name: Game Drives

activities.description: Become one with nature, whilst roaming the plains of Otjiwa. Our professional guides will make you feel at ease on horseback.
activities.name: Horse riding

activities.description: This activity gives you the opportunity to explore nature on your own time. You will be dropped off at one of the look out points, with a snack basket and a radio. Once you are done, you just give the lodge a shout on the radio, and a guide will come get you.
activities.name: Bird-Watching

activities.description: After a hard day's adventuring, relax with a massage, either in the dedicated spa and wellness centre or in the privacy of your room.
activities.name: Spa Treatments

activities.description: Experience the culture and history of the Herero tribe. The Otjiwa guide will transfer you to the village, where the local guide will take you through the history and evolution of their culture, including dress and architecture. Enjoy lunch before returning to the lodge
activities.name: Ombo Village visit

384294.description: Otjiwa Thorn Bush Tented Camp offers an authentic, immersive safari experience where luxury and adventure come together in perfect harmony. Whether guests are seeking a peaceful retreat, an unforgettable family getaway, or an exciting adventure in the wild, Thorn Bush promises an experience that will stay with them long after their journey ends. The camp features 10 spacious tented suites designed for ultimate comfort and privacy. Each suite includes a private wooden deck overlooking the tranquil riverbed, allowing guests to relax and immerse themselves in the stunning surroundings. The suites are equipped with air-conditioning, luxurious en-suite bathrooms, and outdoor showers for a serene nature experience. For families, two suites are designed with adjoining bedrooms and private bathrooms, providing ample space and comfort for all. At Thorn Bush, adventure and nature await. Guests can track rhinos on foot with expert guides, embark on thrilling game drives through the savanna, or take guided night drives to see nocturnal wildlife, ensuring every moment is filled with excitement. For a more tranquil experience, guests can enjoy birdwatching, cultural tours to Ombu Village, horseback riding, and rejuvenating spa treatments.

rooms.description: For families, two suites are designed with adjoining bedrooms and private bathrooms, providing ample space and comfort for all. The spacious tented suites are designed for ultimate comfort and privacy. Each suite features a private wooden deck overlooking the tranquil riverbed, allowing guests to relax and immerse themselves in the stunning surroundings. Enjoy the comfort of air-conditioning, a luxurious en-suite bathroom, and an outdoor shower where guests can embrace nature in the most serene way.
rooms.name: Family Rooms

rooms.description: The spacious tented suites are designed for ultimate comfort and privacy. Each suite features a private wooden deck overlooking the tranquil riverbed, allowing guests to relax and immerse themselves in the stunning surroundings. Enjoy the comfort of air-conditioning, a luxurious en-suite bathroom, and an outdoor shower where clients can embrace nature in the most serene way.
rooms.name: Twin Room

activities.description: Where the sky is not the limit… but only the beginning. Introducing SkyBoma at Mokuti Etosha, Namibia’s first elevated bush dining experience, inspired by tradition, shaped by nature and crafted with care. Dine eight metres above the Etosha Pan under a ceiling of stars, while enjoying world-class Namibian meat and the quiet magic of the wild.
activities.name: SkyBoma

activities.description: Guests can make the most of the landscaped gardens — relaxing in the shade under the trees or at the pool; or visit the exciting reptile park, play a game of tennis, explore the reserve by following one of the marked walking trails, be pampered in the spa or workout at the gym. For the slightly more adventurous, there’s the option of accompanying one of Mokuti’s experienced game guides in an open safari vehicle into Etosha National Park. With its vast number of wildlife species, guests will be sighting and photographing ‘til the sun sets.
activities.name: Game Drives into Etosha National Park

activities.description: The early morning hours promise breathtaking moments in Etosha National Park. A morning bush walk brings you up close and personal with nature. Allow our experienced guides to take you on a journey of discovery filled with entertainment, awakening all your five senses. Afterwards, enjoy a scrumptious breakfast and nature's sweet serenade especially for you
activities.name: Morning Bush Walk including a bush breakfast

activities.description: Join our Conservation Team on a visit to the Ontouka Reptile Park and learn fascinating facts about African reptiles. Book your front row seat at a spectacular crocodiles feeding session! Inhabitants of the park include some of the world’s most dangerous snakes, crocodiles, turtles, goanna, chameleons and many more
activities.name: Ontouka Reptile Park

activities.description:
Luxury salt pool Set beneath the wide Etosha sky, Mokuti Etosha’s pool is a refreshing oasis designed for pure relaxation. Surrounded by tall palm trees, indigenous gardens, and the soft sounds of nature, it’s the perfect place to unwind between game drives or soak up the warm Namibian sun.

Guests can stretch out on comfortable loungers, enjoy chilled drinks from the nearby bar, and take in the peaceful atmosphere that defines the Mokuti experience. Whether you’re enjoying a lazy afternoon, cooling off after exploring Etosha National Park, or watching the golden light settle over the lodge, the pool area offers a tranquil retreat that blends luxury with nature.

A calm, inviting space—crafted for rest, connection, and unforgettable holiday moments.
activities.name: Mokuti Pool

activities.description: A world-class experience is on offer at our Omulilo Spa, a truly indulgent treat for the mind, body and soul. The highly qualified and caring therapists will warmly greet you in this unique sanctuary where you can enjoy serenity and harmony and take a well-deserved break from the stress of daily life. An Omulilo Spa experience is an investment in your well-being, leaving you feeling relaxed, refreshed and re-charged.
activities.name: Omulilo Spa

10525.description:
Arriving at Mokuti – a mere 40 metres from the Etosha Gate - is an experience on its own. A new contemporary safari experience greets you and will leave you speechless as you arrive at the Porte Cochere with its uninterrupted view through the lobby to the pool bar.
Known for its excellent and diverse wildlife encounters, it has become a popular venue for meetings, incentives, weddings and honeymoons. This intimate lodge has 90 rooms and 16 luxury rooms.

The gardens are magnificent, with cool rippling water features that accompany you as you step out of the lobby to the water lounge. Uninterrupted views and gardens that interact with the living spaces create an inviting, friendly, comfortable and relaxing atmosphere, which sets the tone for your stay at Mokuti Etosha.

images.label: pool
images.description: pool

images.label: Mokuti Etosha
images.description: Reception

images.label: Mokuti Etosha
images.description: Itulye Restuarant

images.label: Mokuti Etosha

images.label: Snake park
images.description: snake park

restaurants.description:
Mokuti SkyBoma – A Signature Fireside Experience

Mokuti SkyBoma is an unforgettable open-air dining experience that brings the spirit of Etosha to life under vast Namibian skies. Set around a glowing central firepit, SkyBoma blends authentic bush ambience with modern comfort, creating the perfect setting for relaxed evenings, celebrations, or group gatherings.

Guests can enjoy a taste of true Namibian hospitality, with hearty flavours, crafted cocktails, and warm service that reflects the soul of Mokuti. As the sun sets and stars emerge, the SkyBoma transforms into a magical atmosphere where stories flow, connections deepen, and the beauty of the wilderness surrounds you.

A highlight of any stay at Mokuti, SkyBoma celebrates culture, culinary delight, and the serenity of nature—all brought together in a uniquely memorable experience.
restaurants.name: SkyBoma

restaurants.description: Offering country-style comfort, “home” cuisine with a Namibian touch during breakfast, lunch (dependant on occupancy) and dinner. At night dinners are either a buffet or three course set menus with three selections per course. On certain nights these dinners are served in the garden, under the trees.
restaurants.name: Litulye Dining Room

restaurants.description: End your evening at the Enota Safari Bar. A classic safari bar in an intimate setting, amidst rich luxurious leather couches, where exquisite fine wines and champagnes from all over the world are on offer in an exclusive setting. Whether your pleasure is cocktails, beer, fine wines or exotic spirits, the Enota Safari Bar will delight you. Indulge in a selection of fine imported cigars displayed in a hand-crafted humidor.
restaurants.name: Enota Safari Bar

restaurants.description: The Oshana Pool Bar offers a la carte snacks and light meals, complemented by a selection of teas and coffees throughout the day. In the evenings, unwind with expertly crafted cocktails, premium whiskeys, and fine wines as you reminisce about the incredible wildlife sightings from your day in Etosha National Park. Gather around a crackling fire at Mokuti Etosha and let the magical atmosphere complete your evening.
restaurants.name: Oshana Pool Bar

rooms.description: Our Family Rooms feature a country theme, with stylish modern finishes and generous space to connect with your family. The connected separate room, which can be transformed into a private lounge area with a television, provides a perfect space for family bonding or quiet moments of relaxation.
rooms.name: Standard Family Room

rooms.description:
Step into your private sanctuary in the heart of the bush. Our beautifully appointed Deluxe Rooms offer elevated comfort and seclusion, featuring privacy walls, a private patio with sunbeds, and an invigorating outdoor shower for an immersive connection to nature.

Each en-suite room is tastefully designed with an Etosha-inspired interior, furnished with two single beds that can effortlessly convert into a king-size bed. Whether you're unwinding after a day of adventure or simply soaking up the peaceful surroundings, the Deluxe Room invites you to relax in refined comfort and quiet luxury.
rooms.name: Deluxe Room

rooms.description: Can accommodate max 2 adults & child in cot only. All rooms feature en-suite bathroom with shower, air conditioning, electronic door lock, hair dryer, room safe, telephone, LCD television with satellite TV, Tea/coffee station, mini-fridge, free Wi-Fi.
rooms.name: Standard Room

1644.description:
Situated on a rock terrace half-way up on the slope of Waterberg, the restaurant and eight chalets of Waterberg Plateau Lodge afford grand vistas of the endless Kalahari. Dinner consists of four courses (set menu) with European dishes in the style of Namibian farmhouse cuisine.

The lodge is part of the private nature reserve Waterberg Wilderness, offering a world of experience at the Kalahari's table mountain:
Guests can join guided hikes onto the plateau (8:00) or enjoy an exclusive guided hike just for themselves ("honeymoon sundowner", 16:00). Rhino drives (16:00) and Rhino tracking tours (8:00) offer the opportunity to encounter rhinos and other game at eye-level. There are cultural tours to Hereroland (8:00), not to forget five well-demarcated nature trails including a "Botanical garden" and a History Path with information boards reviving and explaining the past.

images.label: Waterberg Plateau Lodge - Chalets on mountain slope
images.description: The chalets of the Waterberg Plateau Lodge are nestled in the bush on a rock terrace on the slope of the Waterberg.

images.label: Waterberg Wilderness (private nature reserve) - Plateau Hike
images.description: Your hiking guide climbs the plateau with you (approx. 150 m above the plains). He explains plants and animals in the park to you and gives you an insight into the daily life and culture of the Herero people living in this area. Terrific view of the valley or of the plains of the Kalahari.

images.label: Waterberg Plateau Lodge - Chalet terrace
images.description: Living in nature: Each chalet is equipped with a private terrace, offering a view to the plains of the Kalahari and a plunge pool to cool down.

images.label: Waterberg Wilderness (private nature reserve) - Rhino Drive
images.description: Drive through the Kalahari bush savannah in the nature reserve at the foot of the Waterberg. Rub shoulders with rhinos and watch giraffe, zebra, wildebeest and other game.

images.label: Waterberg Plateau Lodge - Rock chalet
images.description: The twin bed chalet with en-suite bathroom and a fireplace offers views to the plains of the Kalahari

images.label: Waterberg Plateau Lodge - Restaurant
images.description: Guests of the lodge are served breakfast and dinner (set menu, three course) while they enjoy the 270-degree-panorama view.

rooms.description: Built from red Waterberg sandstone, the chalet is nestled in the bush on the slope of the mountain and has a private terrace with a plunge pool, providing cooling and attracting birds and small game. The twin-bed room with en-suite bathroom and a sitting area offers views to the endless plains of the Kalahari.
rooms.name: Rock Chalet

1548.description: Situated in Central Namibia, the cosmopolitan city of Windhoek serves as the capital of the country. It is home to an international airport and a plethora of restaurants, shops, entertainment venues and accommodation options. The city is clean, safe and well-organised, with a colonial legacy that is reflected in its many German eateries and shops, and the widespread use of the German language. Windhoek has an interesting mix of historical architecture and modern buildings, many of which are worth a look, including the Alte Feste an old fort, the 1896 Christuskirche Christ Church, and the more contemporary Supreme Court.

country.name: Namibia
country.overview: Located in southwestern Africa, Namibia boasts a well-developed infrastructure, some of the best tourist facilities in Africa, and an impressive list of breathtaking natural wonders. Visitors can explore the capital of Windhoek and discover the lovely coastal town of Swakopmund boasting remnants of the country’s German influence, reflected in the architecture, culture, cuisine and the annual Oktoberfest celebrations. To properly appreciate this extraordinary country, you will have to venture out of the cities to explore the remarkable natural landscapes Namibia has to offer. These include: the impressive Fish River Canyon; the vast Etosha National Park teeming with abundant wildlife, such as lions, desert-adapted elephants and the Hartmann's Mountain Zebra; the hauntingly beautiful Kalahari Desert; and of course the Namib Desert stretching for over 2000 km along the magnificent Atlantic Coast. Namibia is an ideal destination for travellers seeking an unforgettable African experience in a uniquely beautiful untamed wilderness.

country.cuisine:
Food and water safety: 
Can you drink the tap water: Yes 
Fresh fruit and veg: Yes 
Ice: Yes 
Meat: Yes 
Street food: Yes 

Local cuisine: 
Namibian cuisine features a rich blend of German and South African influences, with a focus on grilled meats and fish. Dishes like Biltong (dried, spiced meat), Kapana (grilled meat from street vendors), and Potjiekos (slow-cooked meat and vegetable stew) are popular. Seafood is also abundant along the coast. 

Drinks: 
Popular local drinks include Windhoek Lager and Tafel Lager, locally brewed beers. 

Tipping: customary, usually around 10-15% in restaurants.
country.climate:
Annual rainfall: Varies from under 50 mm in the desert to over 700 mm in the Caprivi Strip 
Average temperature: 20°C 

Summer: 
Average highs: 30°C 
Average lows: 15°C 

Winter: 
Average highs: 20°C 
Average lows: 5°C 

Best time to visit: 
The best time to visit Namibia is during the cooler dry months from May to October when wildlife viewing is at its best in parks like Etosha National Park. The weather is pleasant for exploring the desert landscapes and other outdoor activities.

468.description: Located in the scenic Namib-Naukluft National Park, Sossusvlei is where you will find the iconic red sand dunes of the Namib. The clear blue skies contrast with the giant red dunes to make this one of the most scenic natural wonders of Africa and a photographer's heaven. This awe-inspiring destination is possibly Namibia's premier attraction, with its unique dunes rising to almost 400 metres - making them some of the highest in the world. These iconic dunes come alive in morning and evening light and draw photography enthusiasts from around the globe. Sossusvlei is home to a variety of desert wildlife including oryx, springbok, ostrich and a variety of reptiles. Visitors can climb 'Big Daddy', one of Sossusvlei’s tallest dunes; explore Deadvlei, a white, salt, claypan dotted with ancient trees; or for the more extravagant, scenic flights and hot air ballooning are on offer, followed by a once-in-a-lifetime champagne breakfast amidst these majestic dunes.

country.name: Namibia
country.overview: Located in southwestern Africa, Namibia boasts a well-developed infrastructure, some of the best tourist facilities in Africa, and an impressive list of breathtaking natural wonders. Visitors can explore the capital of Windhoek and discover the lovely coastal town of Swakopmund boasting remnants of the country’s German influence, reflected in the architecture, culture, cuisine and the annual Oktoberfest celebrations. To properly appreciate this extraordinary country, you will have to venture out of the cities to explore the remarkable natural landscapes Namibia has to offer. 471.description: Set along Namibia's spectacularly scenic coast, the seaside town of Swakopmund is known for its wide-open avenues, colonial architecture, and its surrounding otherworldly desert terrain. Founded in 1892 as the main harbour for German South-West Africa, Swakopmund is often described as being more German than Germany. Now a seaside resort town, Swakopmund is the capital of the Skeleton Coast tourism area and has plenty to keep visitors happy. The quirky mix of German and Namibian influences, adventure options, laid-back atmosphere and cool sea breeze make it a very popular Namibian destination. Visitors can look forward to a number of exciting activities including: quad biking, horse riding, paragliding, fishing, sightseeing and fascinating desert tours.

421461.description: Set in the heart of Namibia’s Otjozondjupa region, Otjiwa Game Reserve spans 12,000 hectares of open bushveld, rolling plains and acacia-studded savanna. The reserve exudes a serene and private atmosphere, where wildlife roams freely, and the horizon stretches unbroken beneath vast African skies. Visitors can embark on guided game drives to spot giraffe, zebra, antelope and rhino, or enjoy walking safaris that reveal the subtle rhythms of the bush. At sunrise, the air carries the scent of dry grass and earth, while the calls of birds and distant wildlife punctuate the quiet, a place where Namibia’s wild beauty is both immersive and unforgettable.

48365.description: Located in Northwestern Namibia, Etosha East is a protected sanctuary in the eastern part of the world-renowned Etosha National Park, known as one of the most accessible game reserves in Southern Africa. Etosha East boasts vast open plains scattered with semi-arid savannah grasslands dotted with watering holes and secluded bush camps. An impressive 5000-square-kilometre Etosha salt pan makes up a large area of the eastern side of the park and can even be seen from space. This remote area teems with abundant wildlife such as lions, elephants, black rhinos and giraffes, as well as a variety of birdlife featuring flamingos, ostriches, eagles, hornbills, and owls.

1577.description: Situated in north central Namibia and named for the springs that emanate from its foothills, the Waterberg Plateau National Park is a fascinating geological site featuring compressed sandstone crags, 200 million-year-old dinosaur footprints, and petrified sand dunes. The area’s natural water sources make it far more fertile than its environs, and the park is blessed with a plethora of plant and animal species, including leopards, rhinoceros, vultures, cheetahs, bush babies, ferns and fig trees. Visitors can enjoy a wide selection of activities including: learning about the local Herero culture on an informative village tour, tracking rhino on a guided safari, exploring the Waterberg's 50-kilometre-long and 16-kilometre-wide sandstone plateau, dubbed 'table mountain', and catching a glimpse of the rare and endangered sable and roan antelope.

element_clusters.label: Self Drive from Hosea Kutako International Airport [WDH] to Belvedere Boutique Hotel
Self Drive from Belvedere Boutique Hotel to Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge
Check-in to Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge

element_clusters.description: The Spreetshoogte Pass traverses the Great Escarpment, connecting the Namib Desert to the Khomas Highland. The pass is 50.4 kilometres long, connecting Solitaire to Nauchas and offers stunning vistas of the surrounding landscape. Originally constructed during World War II by farmer Nicolaas Spreeth, the pass was built to facilitate access to his farm, Ubib, located at the base of the escarpment. One of Namibia’s most spectacular views is from the top of the Spreetshoogte Pass, where the land drops off by roughly 2000 metres, creating a natural vantage point overlooking the vast, semi-desert plains and the arid mountains beyond.
element_clusters.label: Spreetshoogte Pass

element_clusters.description: Located in the scenic Namib-Naukluft National Park, Sossusvlei is where you will find the iconic red sand dunes of the Namib. The clear blue skies contrast with the giant red dunes to make this one of the most scenic natural wonders of Africa and a photographer's heaven. This awe-inspiring destination is possibly Namibia's premier attraction, with its unique dunes rising to almost 400 metres - making them some of the highest in the world. These iconic dunes come alive in morning and evening light and draw photography enthusiasts from around the globe. Sossusvlei is home to a variety of desert wildlife including oryx, springbok, ostrich and a variety of reptiles. Visitors can climb 'Big Daddy', one of Sossusvlei’s tallest dunes; explore Deadvlei, a white, salt, claypan dotted with ancient trees; or for the more extravagant, scenic flights and hot air ballooning are on offer, followed by a once-in-a-lifetime champagne breakfast amidst these majestic dunes.
element_clusters.label: Sossusvlei

element_clusters.description: As there is no accommodation at Sossusvlei, visitors to this desert wilderness are likely to end up staying at Sesriem, 65 kilometres away, where camps and lodges serve as a base from which to explore the dunes. Sesriem Canyon, a deep chasm carved through the rocks by water, is a striking natural feature of the area that is best explored on foot. Stony walls rise up sharply on both sides of the canyon, while birds roost in its crags and lizards dart along the ledges. The canyon’s name was coined when early settlers used it as a water source, using six lengths of leather (‘ses riem – six thongs) tied together to lower buckets into the water at the base of canyon.)
element_clusters.label: Sesriem
Self Drive from Wilderness Kulala Desert Lodge to Atlantic Garden Boutique Hotel
Stay at  Atlantic Garden Boutique Hotel

element_clusters.description: Situated in the Khomas region of Central Namibia, the desert town of Solitaire serves as an oasis in the Namib Desert. The town provides an important stop on the way to the ancient dunes of Sossusvlei, allowing travellers to stock up on essentials as they make their way further into the wilderness. Solitaire also has a small bar and is renowned for its famous apple pie - said to be the best in the country. Enjoy a scenic sundowner with views of the spectacular Namib desert, hike and bike through the unspoilt natural surroundings, or visit the wonderful local Cheetah Sanctuary.
element_clusters.label: Solitaire

element_clusters.description: Mysterious and remote, the Kuiseb Canyon was carved aeons ago by the Kuiseb River, which runs mainly underground. This deep fissure is flanked by steep folds of rock on one side and the copper-coloured sand dunes of the Namib Desert on the other, and shelters several wildlife species that are acclimatised to the region’s harsh environment, including hyena, buck and even leopard.
element_clusters.label: Kuiseb Canyon

element_clusters.description: Set along Namibia's spectacularly scenic coast, the seaside town of Swakopmund is known for its wide-open avenues, colonial architecture, and its surrounding otherworldly desert terrain. Founded in 1892 as the main harbour for German South-West Africa, Swakopmund is often described as being more German than Germany. Now a seaside resort town, Swakopmund is the capital of the Skeleton Coast tourism area and has plenty to keep visitors happy. The quirky mix of German and Namibian influences, adventure options, laid-back atmosphere and cool sea breeze make it a very popular Namibian destination. Visitors can look forward to a number of exciting activities including: quad biking, horse riding, paragliding, fishing, sightseeing and fascinating desert tours.
element_clusters.label: Swakopmund
Check-out from Atlantic Garden Boutique Hotel
Self Drive from Atlantic Garden Boutique Hotel to Otjiwa Collection - Thorn Bush Tented Camp

timeline_route.end_content_entity_name: Otjiwa Collection - Thorn Bush Tented Camp

element_clusters.label: Check-in to Otjiwa Collection - Thorn Bush Tented Camp
Check-out from Otjiwa Collection - Thorn Bush Tented Camp
Self Drive from Otjiwa Collection - Thorn Bush Tented Camp to Mokuti Etosha

timeline_route.start_content_entity_name: Otjiwa Collection - Thorn Bush Tented Camp

element_clusters.description: Located just south of the boundary of Etosha National Park in northwestern Namibia, Etosha South makes up the southern region of this wild paradise. The area is comprised of a collection of world class private game reserves. The national park can be accessed via the southern entrance at Andersson’s Gate. Visitors can catch a glimpse of a variety of wildlife including: lion, giraffe, elephant, white and black rhino, and a multitude of plains game. Popular activities include: enjoying an open 4x4 safari with an expert guide, half day or full day drives with the option of a picnic lunch with wine on the full day game drive.
element_clusters.label: Etosha South
Self Drive from Mokuti Etosha to Waterberg Plateau Lodge

element_clusters.description: Situated in north central Namibia and named for the springs that emanate from its foothills, the Waterberg Plateau National Park is a fascinating geological site featuring compressed sandstone crags, 200 million-year-old dinosaur footprints, and petrified sand dunes. The area’s natural water sources make it far more fertile than its environs, and the park is blessed with a plethora of plant and animal species, including leopards, rhinoceros, vultures, cheetahs, bush babies, ferns and fig trees. Visitors can enjoy a wide selection of activities including: learning about the local Herero culture on an informative village tour, tracking rhino on a guided safari, exploring the Waterberg's 50-kilometre-long and 16-kilometre-wide sandstone plateau, dubbed 'table mountain', and catching a glimpse of the rare and endangered sable and roan antelope.
element_clusters.label: Waterberg Plateau National Park
